白鬚神社 | image = Shirahige-shrine.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|28|N|136|00|40|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = [[Sarutahiko Okami]] | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{神社 |名称 = Shirahige Shrine |画像 = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |所在地 = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市}} Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}} |社格 = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} |本殿 = {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |例祭 = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015年4月24日}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == Deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and burial mounds {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7年}} as receiving the junior fourth rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;Location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Nationally designated cultural properties]] [[Category:Japanese Heritage Sites]] [[ja:白鬚神社 (高島市)]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Shirahige Jinja}} ==External links== *[http://shirahigejinja.com Official website] {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Shirahige Jinja
白鬚神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Shirahige-shrine.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|28|N|136|00|40|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = [[Sarutahiko Okami]] | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{神社 |名称 = Shirahige Shrine |画像 = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |所在地 = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市}} Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |祭神 = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}} |社格 = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} |本殿 = {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |例祭 = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015年4月24日}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == Deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and burial mounds {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7年}} as receiving the junior fourth rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Nationally designated cultural properties]] [[Category:Japanese Heritage Sites]] [[ja:白鬚神社 (高島市)]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Shirahige Jinja}} ==External links== *[http://shirahigejinja.com Official website] {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Shirahige Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shirahige Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:白鬚神社]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015年4月24日}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == Deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and burial mounds {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7年}} as receiving the junior fourth rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Nationally designated cultural properties]] [[Category:Japanese {{ill|September 5|en|9月5日}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市}} Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} |本殿 = {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |例祭 = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015年4月24日}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and burial mounds {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7年}} as receiving the junior fourth rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Shirahige Jinja
白鬚神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Shirahige-shrine.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|28|N|136|00|40|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = [[Sarutahiko Okami]] | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{神社 |name = Shirahige Shrine |image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市}} Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} |本殿 = {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015年4月24日}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and burial mounds {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7年}} as receiving the junior fourth rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Nationally designated cultural properties]] [[Category:Japanese Heritage Sites]] [[ja:白鬚神社 (高島市)]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Shirahige Jinja}} ==External links== *[http://shirahigejinja.com Official website] {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Shirahige Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shirahige Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Shirahige Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Shirahige Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Shirahige Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Shirahige Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Shirahige Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:白鬚神社]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Shirahige Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[ja:白鬚神社]] [[en:Shirahige Shrine]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Sarutahiko Faith}}

It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015年4月24日}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and burial mounds {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7年}} as receiving the junior fourth rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shirahige Shrine |image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市}} Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} |本殿 = {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015年4月24日}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and burial mounds {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7年}} as receiving the junior fourth rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Shirahige Jinja
白鬚神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Shirahige-shrine.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|28|N|136|00|40|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = [[Sarutahiko Okami]] | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }}[[ja:白鬚神社]] {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shirahige Shrine |image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市}} Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} |本殿 = {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代type制度}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015年4月24日}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and burial mounds {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7年}} as receiving the junior fourth rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Nationally designated cultural properties]] [[Category:Japanese Heritage Sites]] [[ja:白鬚神社 (高島市)]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Shirahige Jinja}} ==External links== *[http://shirahigejinja.com Official website] {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Shirahige Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shirahige Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Shirahige Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Shirahige Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[en:Shirahige Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Shirahige Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]][[Category:qq 4]] [[Category:qq--qq]] [[en:Shirahige Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:白鬚神社]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:dd]][[Category:Shirahige Shrine]][[Category:dd]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[ja:白鬚神社]] [[en:Shirahige Shrine]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Sarutahiko Faith}} [[Category:New]] It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015年4月24日}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and burial mounds {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7年}} as receiving the junior fourth rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * {{Infobox religious building | name = Shirahige Jinja
白鬚神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Shirahige-shrine.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|28|N|136|00|40|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = [[Sarutahiko Okami]] | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }}[[ja:白鬚神社]] {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shirahige Shrine |image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市}} Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} |本殿 = {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015年4月24日}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and burial mounds {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7年}} as receiving the junior fourth rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Nationally designated cultural properties]] [[Category:Japanese On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015年4月24日}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7年}} as receiving the junior fourth rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * 白鬚神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Shirahige-shrine.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|28|N|136|00|40|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = [[Sarutahiko Okami]] | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }}[[ja:白鬚神社]] {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shirahige Shrine |image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}{{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市}} Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} |本殿 = {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015年4月24日}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|天武天皇}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7年}} as receiving the junior fourth rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Nationally designated cultural properties]] [[Category:Japanese Heritage Sites]] [[ja:白鬚神社 (高島市)]] ==References== {{Commons Category|Shirahige Jinja}} ==External links== *[http://shirahigejinja.com Official website] {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Shirahige Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shirahige Jinja}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7年}} as receiving the junior fourth rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * The shrine is dedicated to [[Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} |本殿 = {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県}}. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社}} under the {{ill|Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015年4月24日}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7年}} as receiving the junior fourth rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture,
