It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft|12=draft}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|12=draft|12=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|12=draft|12=draft}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖|12=draft|12=draft}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|12=draft|12=draft}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|12=draft|12=draft}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=draft|12=draft}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|12=draft|12=draft}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=draft|12=draft}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|12=draft|12=draft}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|12=draft|12=draft}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=draft|12=draft}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|12=draft|12=draft}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|12=draft|12=draft}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7th year|12=draft|12=draft}} as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|12=draft|12=draft}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=draft|12=draft}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|12=draft|12=draft}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|12=draft|12=draft}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8th year|12=draft|12=draft}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * Line|ja|湖西線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * The shrine is dedicated to [[Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. {{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} ==References== {{Commons Category|Shirahige Jinja}} ==External links== *[http://shirahigejinja.com Official website] {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Sarutahiko Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Nationally designated cultural properties]] [[Category:Japanese Heritage Sites]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:白鬚神社]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirahige Shrine]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[ja:白鬚神社]] [[ja:白鬚神社 (高島市)]] [[en:Shirahige Shrine]] [[sn:Shirahige Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shirahige Jinja}} [[Category:Ja import oversized]]

revision.text:
{{otheruses|滋賀県高島市の神社|他の白鬚神社・白髭神社・白髯神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 白鬚神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
境内（左から若宮神社・本殿・拝殿・絵馬殿） |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[高島市]]鵜川215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=白鬚神社}} |祭神 = [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[国史見在社]]
旧[[県社]] |創建 = 伝・第11代[[垂仁天皇]]25年 |本殿 = [[入母屋造]] |別名 = 白鬚大明神・比良明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[5月3日]]（白鬚祭）
[[9月5日]]・[[9月6日|6日]]（秋季大祭） |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=白鬚神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|鳥居の全天球画像}} [[ファイル:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|湖中大鳥居]] '''白鬚神社'''（しらひげじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]鵜川にある[[神社]]。[[国史見在社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。別称は「白鬚大明神」「比良明神」。[[神紋]]は「左三ツ巴」神社由緒書。。 全国にある[[白鬚神社 (曖昧さ回避)|白鬚神社]]の総本社とされる{{Sfn|白鬚神社（平）|1991年}}。[[沖島 (琵琶湖)|沖島]]を背景として[[琵琶湖]]畔に[[鳥居]]を浮かべることから、「近江の[[厳島神社|厳島]]」とも称される。 [[2015年]]（平成27年）[[4月24日]]、「琵琶湖とその水辺景観－ 祈りと暮らしの水遺産 」の構成文化財として[[日本遺産]]に認定される{{Cite web|和書|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=琵琶湖とその水辺景観－祈りと暮らしの水遺産 |publisher=文化庁 |accessdate=2020-09-20 }}。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の1柱{{r|由緒書}}。 * '''[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]''' （さるたひこのみこと、猿田彦大神） 白鬚明神、あるいは比良明神のことであるともいい、[[近江国|近江]]の国の地主の神であるという『[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1913348/1/118 神道大辞典 第二巻]』 p.199。 国史に「比良神」と見える神名が当社を指すとされており、元々の祭神は[[比良山]]の神であるともいわれる{{Sfn|白鬚神社（神々）|1986年|p=348-353}}。 『国史大辞典』には、「現在、祭神は猿田彦命(さるたひこのみこと)であり、『神社啓蒙』『和漢 三才図会』でも祭神を猿田彦とするが、実際は社殿の背後に聳える比良山を神体山とする比良明神である。」とある『国史大辞典』7巻(吉川弘文館、1986年)の「白鬚神社」の項目。 人格神が猿田彦命とされた由来は不詳であるが、猿田彦命は[[水尾神社]]（高島市拝戸）の縁起『三尾神社本土記』にも見えることから、両社の密接な関係も指摘される{{Sfn|白鬚神社（神々）|1986年|p=348-353}}。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === [[ファイル:Mt. Hira-san 01.jpg|thumb|[[比良山]]]] 社伝では、[[垂仁天皇]]（第11代）25年に[[倭姫命]]によって社殿が建てられたのが当社の創建であるとして近江最古の大社としている（一説に再建）{{r|由緒書}}。また[[白鳳]]2年（[[674年]]）には、[[天武天皇]]の勅旨により「比良明神」の号を賜ったとも伝える{{r|由緒書}}。 後述の国史に見える神名「比良神」から、当社の元々の祭祀は[[比良山]]に対するものであったとする説がある{{Sfn|白鬚神社（神々）|1986年|p=348-353}}。 白鬚信仰の多く分布する[[近江国]]や[[武蔵国]]北部・[[筑前国]]には[[渡来人]]が多いことから、それら渡来人が祖神を祀ったことに始まるという説もある{{Sfn|白鬚神社（神々）|1986年|p=348-353}}{{Sfn|日本に残る古代朝鮮 近畿編　段煕鱗　創元社}}{{Sfn|『古代氏族の研究①　和珥氏 中国江南から来た海神族の流れ』青垣出版、2012年}}{{Sfn|街道をゆく 湖西のみち 司馬遼太郎 朝日新聞社 2008}}{{Sfn|滋賀のなかの朝鮮 2003/7/3朴 鐘鳴 P181}}{{Sfn|古代近江の朝鮮 1984/11/1石原 進 丸山 竜平 P209}} 。 当社の周囲には、背後の山中に[[横穴式石室]]（現・末社岩戸社）が残るほか、山頂には[[磐座]]と古墳群が残っている{{Sfn|白鬚神社（神々）|1986年|p=348-353}}。 === 概史 === 国史では[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]7年（[[865年]]）に「比良神」が従四位下の[[神階]]を賜ったとの記載があり、この「比良神」が当社にあたるとされる{{Sfn|白鬚神社（平）|1991年}}。ただし『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]には記載されていないため、当社はいわゆる[[国史見在社]]にあたる。 [[弘安]]3年（[[1280年]]）の比良庄の絵図では「白ヒゲ大明神」と見えるほか（「白鬚」の初出{{Sfn|白鬚神社（神々）|1986年|p=348-353}}）、『[[太平記]]』巻18では「白鬚明神」という記載も見える{{Sfn|白鬚神社（平）|1991年}}。また、[[謡曲]]『白鬚』では当社が舞台とされている{{r|由緒書}}。 その後、[[慶長]]8年（[[1603年]]）には[[豊臣秀頼]]の命により[[片桐且元]]を奉行として本殿（[[重要文化財]]）が再建されたほか、慶長年間（[[1596年]] - [[1615年]]）に摂末社の造営などの境内の整備が行われた{{r|説明板}}。[[慶安]]元年（[[1648年]]）には朱印地として100石を受け、のちには189石余となったという{{Sfn|白鬚神社（平）|1991年}}。 [[明治]]に入り、[[1876年]]（明治9年）に[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列し、[[1922年]]（[[大正]]11年）に[[県社]]に昇格した{{r|神社庁}}。 === 神階 === * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]7年正月18日（[[865年]]）、無位から従四位下 （『[[日本三代実録]]』） - 表記は「比良神」。 == 境内 == [[ファイル:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) shaden.JPG|thumb|社殿（本殿は重要文化財）]] 境内は、慶長年間（[[1596年]] - [[1615年]]）に[[豊臣秀頼]]によって整備が行われている。 * 本殿（[[重要文化財]]） *: [[慶長]]8年（[[1603年]]）の造営。棟札等から、豊臣秀頼の命により[[片桐且元]]を奉行として[[播磨国]]の大工の手で建てられたとされる。間口三間・奥行三間の[[入母屋造]]で、向拝一間を付し、屋根は[[檜皮葺]]である。向拝の[[手挟]]・[[蟇股]]等に[[桃山時代]]の特徴を示している。明治の拝殿造営・接続に伴い、向拝の軒先は切り縮められて[[権現造]]のような複合社殿様式となり、その際に屋根も杮葺から檜皮葺に改められている。この本殿のほか、境内社4殿も同時期の慶長期の造営になる{{r|本殿|神社庁}}境内説明板。。 * 拝殿 *: [[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）再建。間口三間三尺・奥行二間の四棟造{{r|説明板}}[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 白鬚神社]（滋賀県神社庁）。。 * 絵馬殿 *: 旧拝殿。[[寛永]]元年（[[1624年]]）の[[大溝藩|大溝藩主]]の[[分部光信]]による造営。現在は絵馬殿として使用されている{{r|説明板}}。 * 社務所（国[[登録有形文化財]]） *: [[1932年]]（[[昭和]]7年）の造営。[[書院造]]で中世を思わせる造りであるとして、国の登録有形文化財に登録されている{{r|社務所}}。社務所前手水舎横には、[[与謝野鉄幹]]・[[与謝野晶子]]夫妻が当社へ参詣した際に詠んだ「'''しらひげの 神のみまへに わくいづみ'''（上の句：鉄幹）」「'''これをむすべば　ひとの清まる'''（下の句：晶子）」という歌碑が建てられている{{Cite web |url=http://shirahigejinja.com/%e6%b9%96%e4%b8%ad%e9%b3%a5%e5%b1%85%e3%83%bb%e5%a2%83%e5%86%85/keidai/%e5%a2%83%e5%86%85%e3%81%ae%e6%ad%8c%e7%a2%91%e3%83%bb%e5%8f%a5%e7%a2%91/ |title=与謝野寛・晶子　歌碑（手水舎横） |access-date=2024-07-26 |publisher=｜湖中大鳥居・境内｜近江最古の大社　白髭神社}}。 *紫式部歌碑 *: 石段を登った南側には、[[源氏物語]]の作者[[紫式部]]が、[[長徳]]2年（[[996年]]）に越前国司として赴任する父[[藤原為時]]に従って近江を通った時に詠んだ「'''みおの海に 網引く民の てまもなく 立ちゐにつけて 都恋しも'''」という歌碑が建てられている。歌碑には「近江の海にて 三尾が崎といふ所に 網引くを見て」という詞書がある{{Cite web |url=http://shirahigejinja.com/%e6%b9%96%e4%b8%ad%e9%b3%a5%e5%b1%85%e3%83%bb%e5%a2%83%e5%86%85/keidai/%e7%b4%ab%e5%bc%8f%e9%83%a8%e3%80%80%e6%ad%8c%e7%a2%91%ef%bc%88%e7%9f%b3%e6%ae%b5%e4%b8%8a-%e5%8d%97%ef%bc%89/ |title=紫式部　歌碑（石段上 南） |access-date=2024-07-26 |publisher=｜湖中大鳥居・境内｜近江最古の大社　白髭神社}}。 *句碑 *: 与謝野鉄幹・晶子夫妻、紫式部の歌碑以外にも、下記の歌碑が建てられている。 *: 境内旧街道南出口付近に[[松尾芭蕉]]47歳の時の作である「'''四方より 花吹き入て 鳰の湖'''」という句碑{{Cite web |url=http://shirahigejinja.com/%e6%b9%96%e4%b8%ad%e9%b3%a5%e5%b1%85%e3%83%bb%e5%a2%83%e5%86%85/keidai/%e8%8a%ad%e8%95%89%e3%80%80%e5%8f%a5%e7%a2%91%ef%bc%88%e5%a2%83%e5%86%85%e6%97%a7%e8%a1%97%e9%81%93%e5%8d%97%e5%87%ba%e5%8f%a3%e4%bb%98%e8%bf%91%ef%bc%89/ |title=芭蕉　句碑（境内旧街道南出口付近） |access-date=2024-07-26 |publisher=｜湖中大鳥居・境内｜近江最古の大社　白髭神社}}。 *: 石段上の三社南に羽田岳水の「'''比良八荒 沖へ押し出す 雲厚し'''」という句碑{{Cite web |url=http://shirahigejinja.com/%e6%b9%96%e4%b8%ad%e9%b3%a5%e5%b1%85%e3%83%bb%e5%a2%83%e5%86%85/keidai/%e7%be%bd%e7%94%b0%e5%b2%b3%e6%b0%b4%e3%80%80%e5%8f%a5%e7%a2%91%ef%bc%88%e7%9f%b3%e6%ae%b5%e4%b8%8a-%e4%b8%89%e7%a4%be%e5%8d%97%ef%bc%89/ |title=羽田岳水　句碑（石段上 三社南）） |access-date=2024-07-26 |publisher=｜湖中大鳥居・境内｜近江最古の大社　白髭神社}}。 *: 石段上の天満宮南に中野照子の「'''吹き晴れて　藍ふかまれる 湖の光となりて かへりくる舟'''」歌碑{{Cite web |url=http://shirahigejinja.com/%e6%b9%96%e4%b8%ad%e9%b3%a5%e5%b1%85%e3%83%bb%e5%a2%83%e5%86%85/keidai/%e4%b8%ad%e9%87%8e%e7%85%a7%e5%ad%90%e3%80%80%e6%ad%8c%e7%a2%91%ef%bc%88%e7%9f%b3%e6%ae%b5%e4%b8%8a-%e5%a4%a9%e6%ba%80%e5%ae%ae%e5%8d%97%ef%bc%89/ |title=中野照子　歌碑（石段上 天満宮南） |access-date=2024-07-26 |publisher=｜湖中大鳥居・境内｜近江最古の大社　白髭神社}}。 *: 石段上紫式部歌碑の南に松本鷹根の「'''鳥雲に 涙ぐまねば 雲なき日'''」という句碑{{Cite web |url=http://shirahigejinja.com/%e6%b9%96%e4%b8%ad%e9%b3%a5%e5%b1%85%e3%83%bb%e5%a2%83%e5%86%85/keidai/%e6%9d%be%e6%9c%ac%e9%b7%b9%e6%a0%b9%e3%80%80%e5%8f%a5%e7%a2%91%ef%bc%88%e7%9f%b3%e6%ae%b5%e4%b8%8a-%e7%b4%ab%e5%bc%8f%e9%83%a8%e6%ad%8c%e7%a2%91%e5%8d%97%ef%bc%89/ |title=松本鷹根　句碑（石段上 紫式部歌碑南） |access-date=2024-07-26 |publisher=｜湖中大鳥居・境内｜近江最古の大社　白髭神社}}。 === 琵琶湖中の大鳥居 === また大鳥居は[[琵琶湖]]中に建てられ、白髭神社のシンボルとなっている。鳥居について古くは[[弘安]]3年（[[1280年]]）の絵図では陸上に描かれているが、その後の琵琶湖の水位上昇に伴い水中に立つようになったと伝える{{Sfn|白鬚神社（神々）|1986年|p=348-353}}。 『江源武鑑』には、[[永禄]]5年（[[1562年]]）9月に湖中から華表が顕出したという記事がある。これを傍証する史料はないが、『為広越後下向日記』には「鳥井（鳥居）湖ニツクリカクル」とあり、[[延徳]]3年（[[1491年]]）にはすでに鳥居は湖中につくられ隠れているという伝承が存在したことがわかる。 その伝説に基づいて[[1937年]]（[[昭和]]12年）に[[大阪市]][[東区 (大阪市)|東区]]（現・[[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]]）[[道修町]]の薬問屋・小西久兵衛によって鳥居の寄進がなされ、[[1981年]]（昭和56年）に現在に見る鳥居が再建された{{r|由緒書}}。 Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) emaden.JPG|絵馬殿（旧拝殿） Yosano-Kahi.jpg|歌碑 Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) entrance.JPG|境内入り口 === 境内と交通事故 === 前述の大鳥居を見物するために交通量の多い国道161号を横断する参拝者があとをたたず、横断者が直接轢かれる、歩行者に気が付き減速した自動車に後続車が追突するといった[[交通事故]]が相次いで発生している。神社側では国道の横断禁止を呼びかける一方、[[2021年]]には地元の[[ロータリークラブ]]が、神社側に高さ3メートルの撮影台を設置するなど対策は取られているが{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/381994 |title=鳥居撮影のための国道横断やめて　高島・白鬚神社前で死亡事故 |website=中日新聞 |publisher=中日新聞社 |date=2021-12-11 |accessdate=2023-01-08}}撮影台を設置した翌日には、早くも横断中の歩行者が轢かれる事故が起きている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230104110904/https://www.fnn.jp/articles/-/466487 |title=「鳥居」を目指し次々と横断　神社は対策するも死亡事故 |publisher=FNN |date=2023-01-04 |accessdate=2023-01-08}}。 == 摂末社 == [[ファイル:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) wakamiya.JPG|thumb|若宮神社（高島市指定文化財）]] 境内社として11社が鎮座する摂末社の記載は神社由緒書による。。本殿裏の石段上の10社（若宮神社除く10社）は「上の宮」と総称される[http://shirahigejinja.com/uenomiya.html 上の宮]（公式サイト）。。 * 若宮神社（高島市指定有形文化財） - 社殿は慶長期の造営で、豊臣秀頼による再建。一間社[[流造]]の杮葺{{r|説明板}}。 * 皇大神宮（高島市指定有形文化財） - 内宮。社殿は慶長期の造営で、豊臣秀頼による再建。 * 豊受大神宮（高島市指定有形文化財） - 外宮。社殿は慶長期の造営で、豊臣秀頼による再建。 * 八幡三所社（高島市指定有形文化財） - 三社相殿。社殿は慶長期の造営で、豊臣秀頼による再建。 ** 八幡神社 ** 加茂神社 ** 高良神社 * 天満神社 * 浪除稲荷社 * 寿老神社 * 鳴子弁財天社 * 岩戸社 - [[古墳]]の石室前に祠が建てられ、[[天岩戸]]として祀られている。 Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) naiku.JPG|皇大神宮（内宮）（高島市指定有形文化財） Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) geku.JPG|豊受大神宮（外宮）（高島市指定有形文化財） Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) hachiman.JPG|八幡三所社（高島市指定有形文化財） Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidaisha.JPG|（左から）天満・稲荷・寿老・弁財天社 Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) iwatosha.JPG|岩戸社（左）と磐座（右） == 祭事 == 例祭は次の年2回{{r|由緒書}}。 * [[5月3日]]（白鬚祭） * [[9月5日]]・[[9月6日|6日]]（秋季大祭） == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 本殿 附：棟札2枚、こけら板 5枚（建造物） - [[1938年]]（[[昭和]]13年）7月4日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|1532|白鬚神社本殿}}。。 === 登録有形文化財（国登録） === * 社務所 - [[2002年]]（[[平成]]14年）2月14日登録{{国指定文化財等データベース|101|00002641|白鬚神社社務所}}。。 === 高島市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 末社若宮神社本殿（建造物） - 昭和55年12月1日指定{{r|説明板}}。 ** 末社八幡･加茂･高良神社本殿（建造物） ** 末社内宮本殿・外宮本殿（建造物） == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[滋賀県]][[高島市]]鵜川215 ; 交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[西日本旅客鉄道]]（JR西日本）[[湖西線]][[近江高島駅]] ** [[国道161号]]を南へ3km（徒歩約30分）。 ** [[高島市コミュニティバス]][[高島市コミュニティバス#鵜川線|鵜川線]]（要予約）で「白髭神社前」下車（約10分）。 ** 駅構内観光案内所のレンタサイクルで10分。 ; 備考 * 毎年9月5日の秋季大祭の日には、[[北小松駅]]（湖西線）より、[[江若交通]]の臨時バス（[[江若交通#高島今津線|高島今津線]]）が運行される。 * [[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）までは、白鬚神社南方の琵琶湖沿いに[[江若鉄道]]の[[白鬚駅 (滋賀県)|白鬚駅]]があった。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 神社由緒書 * 境内説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=白鬚神社|title=[[府県郷社明治神社誌料]]|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088278/82}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 中』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）82-83コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=[[谷川健一]]|author=橋本鉄男|year=1986|chapter=白鬚神社|title=日本の神々 -神社と聖地- 5 山城・近江|publisher=[[白水社]]|isbn=4560022151|ref={{Harvid|白鬚神社（神々）|1986年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1991|chapter=白鬚神社|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 25 滋賀県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490255|ref={{Harvid|白鬚神社（平）|1991年}}}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat}} * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ 白鬚神社]（公式サイト） * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 白鬚神社]（滋賀県神社庁） * [https://takashima-kanko.jp/spot/2018/06/post_89.html 白鬚神社] - びわ湖高島観光ガイド * [http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/211601.html 比良神]（國學院大學21世紀COEプログラム「神道・神社史料集成」） {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:しらひけしんしや}} [[Category:白鬚神社|*]] [[Category:高島市の神社]] [[Category:国史見在社]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧県社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:日本遺産]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Shirahige Jinja
白鬚神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Shirahige-shrine.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|28|N|136|00|40|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = [[Sarutahiko Okami]] | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shirahige Shrine |image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |本殿 = {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} ==References== {{Commons Category|Shirahige Jinja}} ==External links== *[http://shirahigejinja.com Official website] {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Sarutahiko Faith}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Nationally designated cultural properties]] [[Category:Japanese Heritage Sites]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:白鬚神社]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shirahige Shrine]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[ja:白鬚神社]] [[ja:白鬚神社 (高島市)]] [[en:Shirahige Shrine]] [[sn:Shirahige Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shirahige Jinja}} [[Category:Ja import oversized]] [[Category:jawiki oversized to be reviewed]]

Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} ==References== {{Commons Category|Shirahige Jinja}} ==External links== *[http://shirahigejinja.com Official website] {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Sarutahiko Faith}} that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:白鬚神社]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[ja:白鬚神社]] [[ja:白鬚神社 (高島市)]] [[en:Shirahige Shrine]] [[sn:Shirahige Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Shirahige Jinja}} [[Category:Ja import oversized]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Shirahige Jinja
白鬚神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Shirahige-shrine.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|28|N|136|00|40|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = [[Sarutahiko Okami]] | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shirahige Shrine |image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |本殿 = {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * Line|ja|湖西線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[白鬚神社]] to [[Shirahige Shrine]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{otheruses|滋賀県高島市の神社|他の白鬚神社・白髭神社・白髯神社}} {{神社 |名称 = 白鬚神社 |画像 = [[ファイル:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
境内（左から若宮神社・本殿・拝殿・絵馬殿） |所在地 = [[滋賀県]][[高島市]]鵜川215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=白鬚神社}} |祭神 = [[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]] |神体 = |社格 = [[国史見在社]]
旧[[県社]] |創建 = 伝・第11代[[垂仁天皇]]25年 |本殿 = [[入母屋造]] |別名 = 白鬚大明神・比良明神 |札所等 = |例祭 = [[5月3日]]（白鬚祭）
[[9月5日]]・[[9月6日|6日]]（秋季大祭） |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=白鬚神社|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|鳥居の全天球画像}} [[ファイル:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|湖中大鳥居]] '''白鬚神社'''（しらひげじんじゃ）は、[[滋賀県]][[高島市]]鵜川にある[[神社]]。[[国史見在社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[県社]]。別称は「白鬚大明神」「比良明神」。[[神紋]]は「左三ツ巴」神社由緒書。。 全国にある[[白鬚神社 (曖昧さ回避)|白鬚神社]]の総本社とされる{{Sfn|白鬚神社（平）|1991年}}。[[沖島 (琵琶湖)|沖島]]を背景として[[琵琶湖]]畔に[[鳥居]]を浮かべることから、「近江の[[厳島神社|厳島]]」とも称される。 [[2015年]]（平成27年）[[4月24日]]、「琵琶湖とその水辺景観－ 祈りと暮らしの水遺産 」の構成文化財として[[日本遺産]]に認定される{{Cite web|和書|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=琵琶湖とその水辺景観－祈りと暮らしの水遺産 |publisher=文化庁 |accessdate=2020-09-20 }}。 == 祭神 == 祭神は次の1柱{{r|由緒書}}。 * '''[[サルタヒコ|猿田彦命]]''' （さるたひこのみこと、猿田彦大神） 白鬚明神、あるいは比良明神のことであるともいい、[[近江国|近江]]の国の地主の神であるという『[https://dl.ndl.go.jp/pid/1913348/1/118 神道大辞典 第二巻]』 p.199。 国史に「比良神」と見える神名が当社を指すとされており、元々の祭神は[[比良山]]の神であるともいわれる{{Sfn|白鬚神社（神々）|1986年|p=348-353}}。 『国史大辞典』には、「現在、祭神は猿田彦命(さるたひこのみこと)であり、『神社啓蒙』『和漢 三才図会』でも祭神を猿田彦とするが、実際は社殿の背後に聳える比良山を神体山とする比良明神である。」とある『国史大辞典』7巻(吉川弘文館、1986年)の「白鬚神社」の項目。 人格神が猿田彦命とされた由来は不詳であるが、猿田彦命は[[水尾神社]]（高島市拝戸）の縁起『三尾神社本土記』にも見えることから、両社の密接な関係も指摘される{{Sfn|白鬚神社（神々）|1986年|p=348-353}}。 == 歴史 == === 創建 === [[ファイル:Mt. Hira-san 01.jpg|thumb|[[比良山]]]] 社伝では、[[垂仁天皇]]（第11代）25年に[[倭姫命]]によって社殿が建てられたのが当社の創建であるとして近江最古の大社としている（一説に再建）{{r|由緒書}}。また[[白鳳]]2年（[[674年]]）には、[[天武天皇]]の勅旨により「比良明神」の号を賜ったとも伝える{{r|由緒書}}。 後述の国史に見える神名「比良神」から、当社の元々の祭祀は[[比良山]]に対するものであったとする説がある{{Sfn|白鬚神社（神々）|1986年|p=348-353}}。 白鬚信仰の多く分布する[[近江国]]や[[武蔵国]]北部・[[筑前国]]には[[渡来人]]が多いことから、それら渡来人が祖神を祀ったことに始まるという説もある{{Sfn|白鬚神社（神々）|1986年|p=348-353}}{{Sfn|日本に残る古代朝鮮 近畿編　段煕鱗　創元社}}{{Sfn|『古代氏族の研究①　和珥氏 中国江南から来た海神族の流れ』青垣出版、2012年}}{{Sfn|街道をゆく 湖西のみち 司馬遼太郎 朝日新聞社 2008}}{{Sfn|滋賀のなかの朝鮮 2003/7/3朴 鐘鳴 P181}}{{Sfn|古代近江の朝鮮 1984/11/1石原 進 丸山 竜平 P209}} 。 当社の周囲には、背後の山中に[[横穴式石室]]（現・末社岩戸社）が残るほか、山頂には[[磐座]]と古墳群が残っている{{Sfn|白鬚神社（神々）|1986年|p=348-353}}。 === 概史 === 国史では[[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]7年（[[865年]]）に「比良神」が従四位下の[[神階]]を賜ったとの記載があり、この「比良神」が当社にあたるとされる{{Sfn|白鬚神社（平）|1991年}}。ただし『[[延喜式]]』[[延喜式神名帳|神名帳]]には記載されていないため、当社はいわゆる[[国史見在社]]にあたる。 [[弘安]]3年（[[1280年]]）の比良庄の絵図では「白ヒゲ大明神」と見えるほか（「白鬚」の初出{{Sfn|白鬚神社（神々）|1986年|p=348-353}}）、『[[太平記]]』巻18では「白鬚明神」という記載も見える{{Sfn|白鬚神社（平）|1991年}}。また、[[謡曲]]『白鬚』では当社が舞台とされている{{r|由緒書}}。 その後、[[慶長]]8年（[[1603年]]）には[[豊臣秀頼]]の命により[[片桐且元]]を奉行として本殿（[[重要文化財]]）が再建されたほか、慶長年間（[[1596年]] - [[1615年]]）に摂末社の造営などの境内の整備が行われた{{r|説明板}}。[[慶安]]元年（[[1648年]]）には朱印地として100石を受け、のちには189石余となったという{{Sfn|白鬚神社（平）|1991年}}。 [[明治]]に入り、[[1876年]]（明治9年）に[[近代社格制度]]において[[郷社]]に列し、[[1922年]]（[[大正]]11年）に[[県社]]に昇格した{{r|神社庁}}。 === 神階 === * [[貞観 (日本)|貞観]]7年正月18日（[[865年]]）、無位から従四位下 （『[[日本三代実録]]』） - 表記は「比良神」。 == 境内 == [[ファイル:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) shaden.JPG|thumb|社殿（本殿は重要文化財）]] 境内は、慶長年間（[[1596年]] - [[1615年]]）に[[豊臣秀頼]]によって整備が行われている。 * 本殿（[[重要文化財]]） *: [[慶長]]8年（[[1603年]]）の造営。棟札等から、豊臣秀頼の命により[[片桐且元]]を奉行として[[播磨国]]の大工の手で建てられたとされる。間口三間・奥行三間の[[入母屋造]]で、向拝一間を付し、屋根は[[檜皮葺]]である。向拝の[[手挟]]・[[蟇股]]等に[[桃山時代]]の特徴を示している。明治の拝殿造営・接続に伴い、向拝の軒先は切り縮められて[[権現造]]のような複合社殿様式となり、その際に屋根も杮葺から檜皮葺に改められている。この本殿のほか、境内社4殿も同時期の慶長期の造営になる{{r|本殿|神社庁}}境内説明板。。 * 拝殿 *: [[1879年]]（[[明治]]12年）再建。間口三間三尺・奥行二間の四棟造{{r|説明板}}[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 白鬚神社]（滋賀県神社庁）。。 * 絵馬殿 *: 旧拝殿。[[寛永]]元年（[[1624年]]）の[[大溝藩|大溝藩主]]の[[分部光信]]による造営。現在は絵馬殿として使用されている{{r|説明板}}。 * 社務所（国[[登録有形文化財]]） *: [[1932年]]（[[昭和]]7年）の造営。[[書院造]]で中世を思わせる造りであるとして、国の登録有形文化財に登録されている{{r|社務所}}。社務所前手水舎横には、[[与謝野鉄幹]]・[[与謝野晶子]]夫妻が当社へ参詣した際に詠んだ「'''しらひげの 神のみまへに わくいづみ'''（上の句：鉄幹）」「'''これをむすべば　ひとの清まる'''（下の句：晶子）」という歌碑が建てられている{{Cite web |url=http://shirahigejinja.com/%e6%b9%96%e4%b8%ad%e9%b3%a5%e5%b1%85%e3%83%bb%e5%a2%83%e5%86%85/keidai/%e5%a2%83%e5%86%85%e3%81%ae%e6%ad%8c%e7%a2%91%e3%83%bb%e5%8f%a5%e7%a2%91/ |title=与謝野寛・晶子　歌碑（手水舎横） |access-date=2024-07-26 |publisher=｜湖中大鳥居・境内｜近江最古の大社　白髭神社}}。 *紫式部歌碑 *: 石段を登った南側には、[[源氏物語]]の作者[[紫式部]]が、[[長徳]]2年（[[996年]]）に越前国司として赴任する父[[藤原為時]]に従って近江を通った時に詠んだ「'''みおの海に 網引く民の てまもなく 立ちゐにつけて 都恋しも'''」という歌碑が建てられている。歌碑には「近江の海にて 三尾が崎といふ所に 網引くを見て」という詞書がある{{Cite web |url=http://shirahigejinja.com/%e6%b9%96%e4%b8%ad%e9%b3%a5%e5%b1%85%e3%83%bb%e5%a2%83%e5%86%85/keidai/%e7%b4%ab%e5%bc%8f%e9%83%a8%e3%80%80%e6%ad%8c%e7%a2%91%ef%bc%88%e7%9f%b3%e6%ae%b5%e4%b8%8a-%e5%8d%97%ef%bc%89/ |title=紫式部　歌碑（石段上 南） |access-date=2024-07-26 |publisher=｜湖中大鳥居・境内｜近江最古の大社　白髭神社}}。 *句碑 *: 与謝野鉄幹・晶子夫妻、紫式部の歌碑以外にも、下記の歌碑が建てられている。 *: 境内旧街道南出口付近に[[松尾芭蕉]]47歳の時の作である「'''四方より 花吹き入て 鳰の湖'''」という句碑{{Cite web |url=http://shirahigejinja.com/%e6%b9%96%e4%b8%ad%e9%b3%a5%e5%b1%85%e3%83%bb%e5%a2%83%e5%86%85/keidai/%e8%8a%ad%e8%95%89%e3%80%80%e5%8f%a5%e7%a2%91%ef%bc%88%e5%a2%83%e5%86%85%e6%97%a7%e8%a1%97%e9%81%93%e5%8d%97%e5%87%ba%e5%8f%a3%e4%bb%98%e8%bf%91%ef%bc%89/ |title=芭蕉　句碑（境内旧街道南出口付近） |access-date=2024-07-26 |publisher=｜湖中大鳥居・境内｜近江最古の大社　白髭神社}}。 *: 石段上の三社南に羽田岳水の「'''比良八荒 沖へ押し出す 雲厚し'''」という句碑{{Cite web |url=http://shirahigejinja.com/%e6%b9%96%e4%b8%ad%e9%b3%a5%e5%b1%85%e3%83%bb%e5%a2%83%e5%86%85/keidai/%e7%be%bd%e7%94%b0%e5%b2%b3%e6%b0%b4%e3%80%80%e5%8f%a5%e7%a2%91%ef%bc%88%e7%9f%b3%e6%ae%b5%e4%b8%8a-%e4%b8%89%e7%a4%be%e5%8d%97%ef%bc%89/ |title=羽田岳水　句碑（石段上 三社南）） |access-date=2024-07-26 |publisher=｜湖中大鳥居・境内｜近江最古の大社　白髭神社}}。 *: 石段上の天満宮南に中野照子の「'''吹き晴れて　藍ふかまれる 湖の光となりて かへりくる舟'''」歌碑{{Cite web |url=http://shirahigejinja.com/%e6%b9%96%e4%b8%ad%e9%b3%a5%e5%b1%85%e3%83%bb%e5%a2%83%e5%86%85/keidai/%e4%b8%ad%e9%87%8e%e7%85%a7%e5%ad%90%e3%80%80%e6%ad%8c%e7%a2%91%ef%bc%88%e7%9f%b3%e6%ae%b5%e4%b8%8a-%e5%a4%a9%e6%ba%80%e5%ae%ae%e5%8d%97%ef%bc%89/ |title=中野照子　歌碑（石段上 天満宮南） |access-date=2024-07-26 |publisher=｜湖中大鳥居・境内｜近江最古の大社　白髭神社}}。 *: 石段上紫式部歌碑の南に松本鷹根の「'''鳥雲に 涙ぐまねば 雲なき日'''」という句碑{{Cite web |url=http://shirahigejinja.com/%e6%b9%96%e4%b8%ad%e9%b3%a5%e5%b1%85%e3%83%bb%e5%a2%83%e5%86%85/keidai/%e6%9d%be%e6%9c%ac%e9%b7%b9%e6%a0%b9%e3%80%80%e5%8f%a5%e7%a2%91%ef%bc%88%e7%9f%b3%e6%ae%b5%e4%b8%8a-%e7%b4%ab%e5%bc%8f%e9%83%a8%e6%ad%8c%e7%a2%91%e5%8d%97%ef%bc%89/ |title=松本鷹根　句碑（石段上 紫式部歌碑南） |access-date=2024-07-26 |publisher=｜湖中大鳥居・境内｜近江最古の大社　白髭神社}}。 === 琵琶湖中の大鳥居 === また大鳥居は[[琵琶湖]]中に建てられ、白髭神社のシンボルとなっている。鳥居について古くは[[弘安]]3年（[[1280年]]）の絵図では陸上に描かれているが、その後の琵琶湖の水位上昇に伴い水中に立つようになったと伝える{{Sfn|白鬚神社（神々）|1986年|p=348-353}}。 『江源武鑑』には、[[永禄]]5年（[[1562年]]）9月に湖中から華表が顕出したという記事がある。これを傍証する史料はないが、『為広越後下向日記』には「鳥井（鳥居）湖ニツクリカクル」とあり、[[延徳]]3年（[[1491年]]）にはすでに鳥居は湖中につくられ隠れているという伝承が存在したことがわかる。 その伝説に基づいて[[1937年]]（[[昭和]]12年）に[[大阪市]][[東区 (大阪市)|東区]]（現・[[中央区 (大阪市)|中央区]]）[[道修町]]の薬問屋・小西久兵衛によって鳥居の寄進がなされ、[[1981年]]（昭和56年）に現在に見る鳥居が再建された{{r|由緒書}}。 Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) emaden.JPG|絵馬殿（旧拝殿） Yosano-Kahi.jpg|歌碑 Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) entrance.JPG|境内入り口 === 境内と交通事故 === 前述の大鳥居を見物するために交通量の多い国道161号を横断する参拝者があとをたたず、横断者が直接轢かれる、歩行者に気が付き減速した自動車に後続車が追突するといった[[交通事故]]が相次いで発生している。神社側では国道の横断禁止を呼びかける一方、[[2021年]]には地元の[[ロータリークラブ]]が、神社側に高さ3メートルの撮影台を設置するなど対策は取られているが{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.chunichi.co.jp/article/381994 |title=鳥居撮影のための国道横断やめて　高島・白鬚神社前で死亡事故 |website=中日新聞 |publisher=中日新聞社 |date=2021-12-11 |accessdate=2023-01-08}}撮影台を設置した翌日には、早くも横断中の歩行者が轢かれる事故が起きている{{Cite web|和書|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20230104110904/https://www.fnn.jp/articles/-/466487 |title=「鳥居」を目指し次々と横断　神社は対策するも死亡事故 |publisher=FNN |date=2023-01-04 |accessdate=2023-01-08}}。 == 摂末社 == [[ファイル:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) wakamiya.JPG|thumb|若宮神社（高島市指定文化財）]] 境内社として11社が鎮座する摂末社の記載は神社由緒書による。。本殿裏の石段上の10社（若宮神社除く10社）は「上の宮」と総称される[http://shirahigejinja.com/uenomiya.html 上の宮]（公式サイト）。。 * 若宮神社（高島市指定有形文化財） - 社殿は慶長期の造営で、豊臣秀頼による再建。一間社[[流造]]の杮葺{{r|説明板}}。 * 皇大神宮（高島市指定有形文化財） - 内宮。社殿は慶長期の造営で、豊臣秀頼による再建。 * 豊受大神宮（高島市指定有形文化財） - 外宮。社殿は慶長期の造営で、豊臣秀頼による再建。 * 八幡三所社（高島市指定有形文化財） - 三社相殿。社殿は慶長期の造営で、豊臣秀頼による再建。 ** 八幡神社 ** 加茂神社 ** 高良神社 * 天満神社 * 浪除稲荷社 * 寿老神社 * 鳴子弁財天社 * 岩戸社 - [[古墳]]の石室前に祠が建てられ、[[天岩戸]]として祀られている。 Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) naiku.JPG|皇大神宮（内宮）（高島市指定有形文化財） Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) geku.JPG|豊受大神宮（外宮）（高島市指定有形文化財） Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) hachiman.JPG|八幡三所社（高島市指定有形文化財） Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidaisha.JPG|（左から）天満・稲荷・寿老・弁財天社 Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) iwatosha.JPG|岩戸社（左）と磐座（右） == 祭事 == 例祭は次の年2回{{r|由緒書}}。 * [[5月3日]]（白鬚祭） * [[9月5日]]・[[9月6日|6日]]（秋季大祭） == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 本殿 附：棟札2枚、こけら板 5枚（建造物） - [[1938年]]（[[昭和]]13年）7月4日指定{{国指定文化財等データベース|102|1532|白鬚神社本殿}}。。 === 登録有形文化財（国登録） === * 社務所 - [[2002年]]（[[平成]]14年）2月14日登録{{国指定文化財等データベース|101|00002641|白鬚神社社務所}}。。 === 高島市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 末社若宮神社本殿（建造物） - 昭和55年12月1日指定{{r|説明板}}。 ** 末社八幡･加茂･高良神社本殿（建造物） ** 末社内宮本殿・外宮本殿（建造物） == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[滋賀県]][[高島市]]鵜川215 ; 交通アクセス * 最寄駅：[[西日本旅客鉄道]]（JR西日本）[[湖西線]][[近江高島駅]] ** [[国道161号]]を南へ3km（徒歩約30分）。 ** [[高島市コミュニティバス]][[高島市コミュニティバス#鵜川線|鵜川線]]（要予約）で「白髭神社前」下車（約10分）。 ** 駅構内観光案内所のレンタサイクルで10分。 ; 備考 * 毎年9月5日の秋季大祭の日には、[[北小松駅]]（湖西線）より、[[江若交通]]の臨時バス（[[江若交通#高島今津線|高島今津線]]）が運行される。 * [[1969年]]（[[昭和]]44年）までは、白鬚神社南方の琵琶湖沿いに[[江若鉄道]]の[[白鬚駅 (滋賀県)|白鬚駅]]があった。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}} == 参考文献 == * 神社由緒書 * {{Infobox religious building | name = Shirahige Jinja
白鬚神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Shirahige-shrine.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|28|N|136|00|40|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = [[Sarutahiko Okami]] | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shirahige Shrine |image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |本殿 = {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} ==References== {{Commons Category|Shirahige Jinja}} ==External links== *[http://shirahigejinja.com Official website] {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Sarutahiko Faith}} {{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * Line|ja|湖西線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} ==References== {{Commons Category|Shirahige Jinja}} ==External links== *[http://shirahigejinja.com Official website] {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Sarutahiko Faith}} outlinks later]] [[Category:Shirahige Shrine]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[ja:白鬚神社]] [[ja:白鬚神社 (高島市)]] [[en:Shirahige Shrine]] [[sn:Shirahige Shrine]]

The shrine is dedicated to [[Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shirahige Shrine |image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}{{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |本殿 = {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and its kami. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}} as receiving the junior fourth Rank. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an
