It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa|qq=}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukushima Shrine|qq=}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|qq=}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Province|qq=}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|qq=}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|qq=}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/865|qq=}} as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyori|qq=}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1603|qq=}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irimoya-zukuri|qq=}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1932|qq=}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2002|qq=}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|qq=}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi-Takashima Station|qq=}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosei Line|qq=}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto {{Infobox religious building | name = Shirahige Jinja
白鬚神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Shirahige-shrine.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|28|N|136|00|40|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = [[Sarutahiko Okami]] | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shirahige Shrine |image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima|qq=}} Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}}

{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 5|qq=}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 6|qq=}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima|qq=}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa|qq=}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukushima Shrine|qq=}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|qq=}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Province|qq=}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|qq=}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|qq=}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/865|qq=}} as receiving the junior fourth Rank. Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}} |本殿 = {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irimoya-zukuri|qq=}} |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 3|qq=}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 5|qq=}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 6|qq=}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima|qq=}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa|qq=}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukushima Shrine|qq=}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|qq=}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Province|qq=}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|qq=}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|qq=}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/865|qq=}} as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyori|qq=}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1603|qq=}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irimoya-zukuri|qq=}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1932|qq=}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2002|qq=}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|qq=}} ;Transportation * From {{ill|Ōmi-Takashima Station|ja|近江高島駅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi-Takashima Station|qq=}} on the JR {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosei Line|qq=}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} ==References== {{Commons Category|Shirahige Jinja}} ==External links== *[http://shirahigejinja.com Official website] {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Shinto-stub}} It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. [[ja:白鬚神社]] [[ja:白鬚神社 (高島市)]] [[en:Shirahige Shrine]] [[sn:Shirahige Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Shirahige Jinja
白鬚神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Shirahige-shrine.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|28|N|136|00|40|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = [[Sarutahiko Okami]] | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shirahige Shrine |image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}{{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima|qq=}} Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}}

{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 5|qq=}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 6|qq=}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Takashima|ja|高島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takashima|qq=}}, {{ill|Shiga Prefecture|ja|滋賀県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiga Prefecture|qq=}}. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the {{ill|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|ja|近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|1=Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines|13=User:Immanuelle/Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|qq=}}. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in {{ill|Lake Biwa|ja|琵琶湖|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lake Biwa|qq=}}, resembling the famous {{ill|Itsukushima Shrine|ja|厳島神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukushima Shrine|qq=}}. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|qq=}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōmi Province|qq=}}.'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|qq=}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of {{ill|Emperor Suinin|ja|垂仁天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Suinin|qq=}} by {{ill|Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|ja|倭姫命|1=Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato-hime-no-Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamatohime-no-mikoto|qq=}} and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|qq=}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the {{ill|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|ja|日本三代実録|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|qq=}} in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/865|qq=}} as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|1=Keichō|2=ja|3=慶長|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyori|qq=}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1603|qq=}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irimoya-zukuri|qq=}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1932|qq=}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2002|qq=}}.{{Cite In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}}. During the {{ill|Keichō|1=Keichō|2=ja|3=慶長|ja|慶長|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keichō|qq=}} era, {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyori|ja|豊臣秀頼|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotomi Hideyori|qq=}} commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1603|qq=}}. The main hall is designated as an {{ill|Important Cultural Property|ja|重要文化財|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)|2=ja|3=重要文化財|lt=Important Cultural Property|qq= (Japan)|4=ko|5=일본의_중요문화재|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Cultural Property (Japan)|qq=}}. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an {{ill|Irimoya-zukuri|ja|入母屋造|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Irimoya-zukuri|qq=}} style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1932|qq=}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2002|qq=}}.{{Cite {{ill|Kosei Line|ja|湖西線|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kosei Line|qq=}}, it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the {{Infobox religious building | name = Shirahige Jinja
白鬚神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Shirahige-shrine.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|28|N|136|00|40|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = [[Sarutahiko Okami]] | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shirahige Shrine |image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |本殿 = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 3|qq=}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 5|qq=}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 6|qq=}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], resembling the famous [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|qq=}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]].'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|qq=}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|qq=}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/865|qq=}} as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] era, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1603|qq=}}. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1932|qq=}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2002|qq=}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|qq=}} ;Transportation * From [[:en:Ōmi-Takashima Station|Ōmi-Takashima Station]] on the JR [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]], it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|qq=}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/865|qq=}} as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] era, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1603|qq=}}. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1932|qq=}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2002|qq=}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|qq=}} ;Transportation * From [[:en:Ōmi-Takashima Station|Ōmi-Takashima Station]] on the JR [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]], it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Shirahige Jinja
白鬚神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Shirahige-shrine.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|28|N|136|00|40|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = [[Sarutahiko Okami]] | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shirahige Shrine |image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |本殿 = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 3|qq=}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 5|qq=}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 6|qq=}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], resembling the famous [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|qq=}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]].'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|qq=}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|qq=}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/865|qq=}} as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] era, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1603|qq=}}. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1932|qq=}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2002|qq=}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Station]] on the JR [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]], it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1932|qq=}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2002|qq=}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|qq=}} ;Transportation * From [[:en:Ōmi-Takashima Station|Ōmi-Takashima Station]] on the JR [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]], it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Shirahige Jinja
白鬚神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Shirahige-shrine.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|28|N|136|00|40|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = [[Sarutahiko Okami]] | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shirahige Shrine |image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |本殿 = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 3|qq=}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 5|qq=}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 6|qq=}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], resembling the famous [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|qq=}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]].'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|qq=}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|qq=}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/865|qq=}} as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] era, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1603|qq=}}. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1932|qq=}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2002|qq=}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|qq=}} ;Transportation * From [[:en:Ōmi-Takashima Station|Ōmi-Takashima Station]] on the JR [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]], it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} ==References== {{Commons Category|Shirahige Jinja}} ==External links== *[http://shirahigejinja.com Official website] {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Sarutahiko Faith}} {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2002|qq=}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|qq=}} ;Transportation * From [[:en:Ōmi-Takashima Station|Ōmi-Takashima Station]] on the JR [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]], it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * revision.text:
{{Infobox religious building | name = Shirahige Jinja
白鬚神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = Shirahige-shrine.jpg | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|35|16|28|N|136|00|40|E|source:kolossus-jawiki|display=title,inline}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = [[Sarutahiko Okami]] | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in [[Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[Shiga Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shirahige Shrine |image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |本殿 = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 3|qq=}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 5|qq=}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 6|qq=}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|qq=}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], resembling the famous [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|qq=}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]].'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|qq=}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|qq=}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|qq=}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|qq=}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in {{ill|865|ja|貞観7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/865|qq=}} as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|qq=}}. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] era, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in {{ill|1603|ja|慶長8年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1603|qq=}}. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in {{ill|1932|ja|昭和7年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1932|qq=}}, designated in {{ill|2002|ja|平成14年|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2002|qq=}}.{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|qq=}} ;Transportation * From [[:en:Ōmi-Takashima Station|Ōmi-Takashima Station]] on the JR [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]], it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Normdaten}} ==References== {{Commons Category|Shirahige Jinja}} ==External links== *[http://shirahigejinja.com Official website] {{Reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Shinto-stub}} {{Sarutahiko Faith}} {{translated page|ja|白鬚神社|version=104460235|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Japanese Heritage Sites]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Japan]] [[Category:Kokushi genzaisha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:confused probably misplaced articles linked from page Torinoichi probably under wrong names recheck that page and its outlinks later]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[ja:白鬚神社]] [[ja:白鬚神社 (高島市)]] [[en:Shirahige Shrine]] [[sn:Shirahige Shrine]] [[Category:日本遺産]] [[Category:Former Prefectural Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:滋賀県の重要文化財]] [[Category:白鬚神社]] [[Category:重要文化財 (建造物)]]
