白鬚神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.27444444|N|136.01111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style ={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Takashima|WD=Q845794}} }} {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[:en:Sarutahiko Okami|Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shirahige Shrine |image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |本殿 = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 3|qq=}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 5|qq=}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 6|qq=}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], resembling the famous [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|qq=}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]].'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|1=Hira Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8524042}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|1=Hira Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8524042}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in [[:en:865|865]] as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] era, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in [[:en:1603|1603]]. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in [[:en:1932|Showa 7]], designated in [[:en:2002|Heisei 14]].{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ;Transportation * From [[:en:Ōmi-Takashima Station|Ōmi-Takashima Station]] on the JR [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]], it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==References== {{Commons Category|Shirahige Jinja}} ==External links== *[http://shirahigejinja.com Official website] {{Reflist}} Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], resembling the famous [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|qq=}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]].'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|1=Hira Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8524042}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|1=Hira Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8524042}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in [[:en:865|865]] as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] era, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in [[:en:1603|1603]]. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in [[:en:1932|Showa 7]], designated in [[:en:2002|Heisei 14]].{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ;Transportation * From [[:en:Ōmi-Takashima Station|Ōmi-Takashima Station]] on the JR [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]], it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [[sn:commons:Category:Shirahige Jinja]][[en:Shirahige Shrine]][[fr:Shirahige-jinja]][[ja:白鬚神社]][[zh:白鬚神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q705303]] Wikidata name: Shirahige Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Shirahige Jinja
白鬚神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.27444444|N|136.01111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style ={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Takashima|WD=Q845794}} }} {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[:en:Sarutahiko Okami|Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shirahige Shrine |image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |本殿 = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 3|qq=}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 5|qq=}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 6|qq=}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], resembling the famous [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|qq=}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]].'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|1=Hira Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8524042}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|1=Hira Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8524042}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in [[:en:865|865]] as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] era, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in [[:en:1603|1603]]. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in [[:en:1932|Showa 7]], designated in [[:en:2002|Heisei 14]].{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ;Transportation * From [[:en:Ōmi-Takashima Station|Ōmi-Takashima Station]] on the JR [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]], it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==References== {{Commons Category|Shirahige Jinja}} ==External links== *[http://shirahigejinja.com Official website] {{Reflist}} Shrines in Shiga Prefecture]] [[Category:Important Cultural Property of Japan in Shiga prefecture]] [[Category:Shirahige-jinja (worship)]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] {{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 5|qq=}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 6|qq=}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], resembling the famous [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|qq=}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]].'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|1=Hira Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8524042}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|1=Hira Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8524042}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in [[:en:865|865]] as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] era, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in [[:en:1603|1603]]. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in [[:en:1932|Showa 7]], designated in [[:en:2002|Heisei 14]].{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 5|qq=}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 6|qq=}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], resembling the famous [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]. Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |本殿 = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 3|qq=}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 5|qq=}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 6|qq=}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], resembling the famous [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|qq=}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]].'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|1=Hira Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8524042}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|1=Hira Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8524042}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in [[:en:865|865]] as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] era, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in [[:en:1603|1603]]. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in [[:en:1932|Showa 7]], designated in [[:en:2002|Heisei 14]].{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ;Transportation * From [[:en:Ōmi-Takashima Station|Ōmi-Takashima Station]] on the JR [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]], it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * 白鬚神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.27444444|N|136.01111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style ={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Takashima|WD=Q845794}} }} {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[:en:Sarutahiko Okami|Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[commons:Category:Shirahige Jinja]][[en:Shirahige Shrine]][[fr:Shirahige-jinja]][[ja:白鬚神社]][[zh:白鬚神社]] [[commons:Category:Shirahige Jinja]][[en:Shirahige Shrine]][[fr:Shirahige-jinja]][[ja:白鬚神社]][[zh:白鬚神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shirahige Jinja]][[en:Shirahige Shrine]][[fr:Shirahige-jinja]][[ja:白鬚神社]][[zh:白鬚神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q705303]] Wikidata name: Shirahige Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Shirahige Jinja
白鬚神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.27444444|N|136.01111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style ={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Takashima|WD=Q845794}} }} {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[:en:Sarutahiko Okami|Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shirahige Shrine |image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |本殿 = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 3|qq=}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 5|qq=}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 6|qq=}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], resembling the famous [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|qq=}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]].'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|1=Hira Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8524042}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|1=Hira Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8524042}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in [[:en:865|865]] as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] era, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in [[:en:1603|1603]]. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in [[:en:1932|Showa 7]], designated in [[:en:2002|Heisei 14]].{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ;Transportation * From [[:en:Ōmi-Takashima Station|Ōmi-Takashima Station]] on the JR [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]], it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==References== {{Commons Category|Shirahige Jinja}} ==External links== *[http://shirahigejinja.com Official website] {{Reflist}} In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|1=Hira Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8524042}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in [[:en:865|865]] as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] era, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in [[:en:1603|1603]]. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in [[:en:1932|Showa 7]], designated in [[:en:2002|Heisei 14]].{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ;Transportation * From [[:en:Ōmi-Takashima Station|Ōmi-Takashima Station]] on the JR [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]], it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – [[commons:Category:Shirahige Jinja]][[en:Shirahige Shrine]][[fr:Shirahige-jinja]][[ja:白鬚神社]][[zh:白鬚神社]] [[commons:Category:Shirahige Jinja]][[en:Shirahige Shrine]][[fr:Shirahige-jinja]][[ja:白鬚神社]][[zh:白鬚神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Shirahige Jinja]][[en:Shirahige Shrine]][[fr:Shirahige-jinja]][[ja:白鬚神社]][[zh:白鬚神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q705303]] Wikidata name: Shirahige Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:25, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Shirahige Jinja
白鬚神社 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG | caption = | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.27444444|N|136.01111111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = Shirahige Jinja | deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|WD=Q3090037}} | founded_by = | established = 5 BCE | date_destroyed = | location = 215 Ukawa, [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|http://shirahigejinja.com/}} | architecture_style ={{ill|East Asian hip-and-gable roof|WD=Q855025}} | festival = | leadership = | location = {{ill|Takashima|WD=Q845794}} }} {{nihongo|'''Shirahige Jinja'''|白鬚神社}} is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] in [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]], [[:en:Japan|Japan]]. The shrine is dedicated to [[:en:Sarutahiko Okami|Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6. [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The floating torii at Shirahige]] {{otheruses|Shirahige Shrine in Takashima, Shiga Prefecture|Other Shirahige Shrines}} {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name = Shirahige Shrine |image = [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) keidai.JPG|280px]]
Grounds (from left: Wakamiya Shrine, Main Hall, Worship Hall, Ema Hall) |location = [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]][[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]] Ukawa 215 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E|region:JP-25_type:landmark|display=inline,title|name=Shirahige Shrine}} |deity = {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}} |type = {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}
Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |本殿 = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 3|qq=}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 5|qq=}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 6|qq=}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], resembling the famous [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|qq=}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]].'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|1=Hira Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8524042}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|1=Hira Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8524042}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in [[:en:865|865]] as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] era, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in [[:en:1603|1603]]. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in [[:en:1932|Showa 7]], designated in [[:en:2002|Heisei 14]].{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ;Transportation * From [[:en:Ōmi-Takashima Station|Ōmi-Takashima Station]] on the JR [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]], it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * [http://shirahigejinja.com/ Shirahige Shrine Official Website] * [http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=1404 Shirahige Shrine – Shiga Prefecture Shrine Association] {{Shinto Horizontal}} ==References== {{Commons Category|Shirahige Jinja}} ==External links== *[http://shirahigejinja.com Official website] {{Reflist}} Former {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} |創建 = Traditionally during the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] |本殿 = [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] |別名 = Shirahige Daimyōjin, Hira Daimyōjin |festival = {{ill|May 3|en|5月3日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/May 3|qq=}} (Shirahige Festival)
{{ill|September 5|en|9月5日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 5|qq=}} and {{ill|September 6|en|9月6日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/September 6|qq=}} (Autumn Grand Festival) |地図 = {{location map|Japan Shiga|lat_deg=35|lat_min=16|lat_sec=28.25|lon_deg=136|lon_min=0|lon_sec=39.92|label=Shirahige Shrine|position=top|width=210|float=center|caption=|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=20}}{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=11|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=210|coord={{coord|35|16|28.25|N|136|0|39.92|E}}}} }} {{panoViewer|Shirahige-jinja Takashima 360-degree.jpg|Panoramic view of the torii}} [[File:Shirahige-jinja (Takashima) otorii.JPG|thumb|The large torii in Lake Biwa]] '''Shirahige Shrine''' (白鬚神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Takashima, Shiga|Takashima]], [[:en:Shiga Prefecture|Shiga Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}} and formerly classified as a {{ill|Prefectural Shrine|ja|県社|1=Prefectural Shrine|WD=Q90139226}}{{efn|Ken-sha|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Prefectural Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}} under the [[:en:Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|Modern system of Ranked Shinto shrines]]. Known as "Shirahige Daimyōjin" or "Hira Daimyōjin," the shrine's crest is the "Left Mitsudomoe" (三ツ巴).Shrine history document. It is regarded as the head shrine of all Shirahige Shrines in Japan {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}} and is often called the "Itsukushima of Ōmi" due to its large torii standing in [[:en:Lake Biwa|Lake Biwa]], resembling the famous [[:en:Itsukushima Shrine|Itsukushima Shrine]]. On {{ill|April 24, 2015|en|2015th year 4月24日|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/April 24, 2015|qq=}}, it was designated as part of the Japanese Heritage site "Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life." {{Cite web|ja|url=https://japan-heritage.bunka.go.jp/ja/stories/story008/ |title=Lake Biwa and its Waterside Scenery – Water Heritage of Prayer and Life |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=2020-09-20}} == deity == The enshrined deity is {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, also referred to as Shirahige Daimyōjin or Hira Daimyōjin, considered the local land deity of [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]].'{{Cite book|和書|title=Shintō Daijiten, Volume 2|year=|publisher=Kōbunsha}}' p.199 While the current enshrined deity is identified as {{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja|猿田彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko Ōkami|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, it is believed that the original worship focused on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|1=Hira Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8524042}} and its kami. Historical texts such as the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] refer to "Hira no Kami," believed to be this shrine's deity {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. == History == === Founding === According to legend, the shrine was established in the 25th year of [[:en:Emperor Suinin|Emperor Suinin]] by [[:en:Yamatohime-no-mikoto|Yamatohime-no-mikoto]] and is considered the oldest grand shrine in Ōmi Province {{r|ShrineHistory}}. In the 2nd year of the Hakuhō era (674), {{ill|Emperor Tenmu|1=Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q335181}} reportedly bestowed the title "Hira Daimyōjin" upon it {{r|ShrineHistory}}. The shrine's original worship is thought to have centered on {{ill|Mount Hira|ja|比良山|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mount Hira|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 比良山地|1=Hira Mountains|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8524042}}, evidenced by its geographic features and remains such as stone chambers and Kofuns {{Sfn|Shirahige Shrine (Heibonsha)|1991}}. === Historical Developments === The shrine is mentioned in the [[:en:Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku|Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]] in [[:en:865|865]] as receiving the junior fourth Rank. However, it is not listed in the {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}}, making it a {{ill|Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja|国史見在社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokushi Genzai-sha|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=changed title based on wikidata|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 社格|comment=enwiki is a redirect|1=shrine rank|WD=Q10444029}}. During the [[:en:Keichō|Keichō]] era, [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyori|Toyotomi Hideyori]] commissioned extensive reconstructions, including the current main hall, completed in [[:en:1603|1603]]. The main hall is designated as an [[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]]. == Cultural Properties == === Important Cultural Properties (Nationally Designated) === * **Main Hall**: Built in 1603, featuring an [[:en:East Asian hip-and-gable roof|Irimoya-zukuri]] style.{{Cite database|ID=1021532|Title=Shirahige Shrine Main Hall}} === Registered Tangible Cultural Properties === * **Shrine Office**: Built in [[:en:1932|Showa 7]], designated in [[:en:2002|Heisei 14]].{{Cite database|ID=10100002641|Title=Shirahige Shrine Office}} == Access == ;location * {{ill|215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja|滋賀県高島市鵜川215|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/215 Ukawa, Takashima, Shiga Prefecture, Japan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ;Transportation * From [[:en:Ōmi-Takashima Station|Ōmi-Takashima Station]] on the JR [[:en:Kosei Line|Kosei Line]], it is a 30-minute walk south (3 km). * Take the Takashima City Community Bus (reservation required) on the Ukawa Line to "Shirahige Shrine-mae" (10 minutes). * Rent a bicycle from the station's tourism information center (10 minutes). == External Links == * The shrine is dedicated to [[:en:Sarutahiko Okami|Sarutahiko Okami]]. It is the head shrine of the Shirahige Shrines around the country.{{cite web|url=http://shirahigejinja.com/ |title=白鬚神社ＨＰ |publisher=Shirahigejinja.com |date= |accessdate=2011-10-17}} The shrine's annual festivals are on May 3 and September 5-6.
